zh.Ormesby Saint Margaret, Norfolk, England: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 瑪格麗特
英國諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 瑪格麗特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Benjamin Moulton於大約1592年出生在英國諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 瑪格麗特時，他的父親John Moulton31歲，母親Anne Taylor33歲。他於大約1612年與Mary在英國英格蘭諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 瑪格麗特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1623年7月15日之前在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.Ormesby Saint Margaret, Norfolk, England: 英国英格兰諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 瑪格麗特
英国諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 瑪格麗特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Benjamin Moulton于大约1592年出生在英国諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 瑪格麗特时，他的父亲John Moulton31岁，母亲Anne Taylor33岁。他于大约1612年与Mary在英国英格兰諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 瑪格麗特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1623年7月15日之前在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Moulton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Cheshire and Lancashire): habitational name from one or more of the places called Moulton (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, Northamptonshire, Staffordshire, Suffolk, North Yorkshire), most of which are named with the Old English personal name Mūl(a), the Old Norse personal name Múli, or Old English mūl ‘mule’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Molden
meanings: metronymic from the Middle English female personal name Mold, a form of Maud, with the characteristically West Midland genitival suffix -en.

nameMeanings.name: Benjamin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the youngest of the twelve sons of Jacob. His mother Rachel died in giving birth to him, and in her last moments she named him Benoni, meaning ‘son of my sorrow’. His father, however, did not wish him to bear such an ill-omened name, and renamed him Benyamin (Genesis 35:16–18; 42:4). This means either ‘son of the right hand’ or more likely ‘son of the south’ (Hebrew yamin can also mean ‘south’), since Benjamin was the only child of Jacob born in Canaan and not in Mesopotamia to the north. Another tradition is that the second element of the name is a variant of the Hebrew plural noun yamim, which means ‘days’ but is used idiomatically to mean ‘year’ or ‘years’. The name would then mean ‘son of (my) old age’ and refer to the fact that Benjamin was Jacob's youngest child. In the Middle Ages the name was often given to sons whose mothers had died in childbirth. Today it has no such unfortunate associations and it grew enormously in popularity following the release of the film The Graduate ( 1967 ), in which Dustin Hoffman played the role of Benjamin Braddock . It is used in Scotland as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Beathan .

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Benjamin Moulton was born about 1592, in Ormesby Saint Margaret, Norfolk, England, his father, John Moulton, was 31 and his mother, Anne Taylor, was 33. He married Mary about 1612, in Ormesby St Margaret, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died before 15 July 1623, in his hometown.
